I INTRODUCTION 
I.1 Product name  
OMI_EXTREME_WAVE_BALTIC_swh_mean_and_anomaly_obs 
OMI_EXTREME_WAVE_NORTHWESTSHELF_swh_mean_and_anomaly_obs 
OMI_EXTREME_WAVE_IBI_swh_mean_and_anomaly_obs 
OMI_EXTREME_WAVE_MEDSEA_swh_mean_and_anomaly_obs 
I.2 Indicator evaluation method 
The EXTREME WAVE indicators are based on the computation of the 99th and 
the 1st percentiles (that represent approximately the 1% highest and lowest 
values, respectively) from in situ data (observations), following the same 
approach applied by Woodworth and Blackman (2004) to extrem e sea levels. 
This method determines changes in the frequency distribution of the measured 
variables. 
All the data used for the computation of these indicators have been 
downloaded from Copernicus Marine Service In Situ Thematic Assembly Centre 
(In Situ TAC) products. The analysis is performed for the following variable: 
significant wave height (swh) measured by in situ buoys.  
The periods selected are based on our analysis of the data available in the 
different In Situ TAC  regions that fulfilled specific  criteria of data completeness 
and quality (Pérez Gómez et al 2016, Pérez Gómez et al 2018). Buoys were 
selected according to consolidated time series of hourly data in Copernicus 
Marine Service for the period 2000 to the target year. Within these periods,  a 
minimum number of 10 years with completeness index larger than 70% is 
required for a station to be included.  These numbers can be reduced to  8 
years and  60% in case that the coverage is not enough  to make the OMIs 
properly representative. These data wer e then used to compute annually the 
99th and 1st percentile levels at each station. 
More details can be found in the Copernicus Marine Service Ocean State 
Report (Pérez Gómez et al., 2016; Pérez Gómez et al., 2018).

catalog_entry.summary: This Quality Information Document describes the evaluation method for Ocean Monitoring Indicators related to extreme wave heights, focusing on significant wave height data from in situ buoys.
